projects.title: CourseLENS
projects.description: Built a governed AI reasoning system that controls exactly what an LLM can and cannot say based on verified course material. The core idea was replacing unpredictable LLM behavior with a 4-stage policy engine that routes every query deterministically and logs every decision. Retrieval is anchored to a parent-child vector store with curriculum-gated boundaries so the system only reasons over content a student has actually covered. Validated under adversarial stress testing with 100% policy compliance and 96.15% Ragas-scored faithfulness.

projects.title: FinAgent
projects.description: Built a two-stage pipeline that processes 72 SEC 10-K filings by separating deterministic NLP extraction from generative reasoning. A multi-threaded ingestion engine parallelizes numerical and textual data extraction, cutting LLM token costs by 98% and reducing latency to sub-90 seconds. A normalization layer deduplicates XBRL tags and aligns 5-year time-series across 16 companies. All LLM outputs are schema-validated via Pydantic, achieving 95% plus faithfulness across 1,500 plus structured signals.

projects.title: AudioSEEK
projects.description: Built a production RAG platform that converts long-form audio into a searchable knowledge base and delivers timestamp-aligned answers under 15 seconds. The ingestion pipeline runs async transcription with an admin dashboard for real-time status monitoring, restart controls, and completion notifications. A hybrid SQL-FAISS store enforces temporal constraints to prevent lookahead bias and ensure data integrity. Deployed as Dockerized GKE microservices via Terraform with MLflow instrumented for latency tracking and production drift detection.

projects.title: Early Product Success Prediction
projects.tagline: Demand Signal Modeling and Feature Engineering
projects.description: Built a PySpark pipeline that transforms raw review data into a model-ready dataset for predicting 28-day product launch success. Engineered 40 structured signals across rating trends, sentiment polarity, and review velocity, then trained XGBoost models under A/B ablation across single and multimodal inputs. Achieved Lift@100 of 2.6x over baseline and Precision@100 of 0.65, outperforming all single-modality models. Added SHAP explainability to surface the key drivers behind every prediction.

projects.title: RoadSense
projects.description: Built an accident severity prediction system to help prioritize emergency response. Consolidated a 52K-record dataset from 4 tables and engineered 50 features across weather, road conditions, time, and location. Trained an XGBoost model under 17:1 class imbalance using class weights and threshold tuning to maximize recall on severe accidents. Converted model outputs into a lethality risk score for real-time dispatch optimization.

projects.title: Effects of Exogenous Factors in Traffic Forecasting
projects.tagline: Urban Traffic Flow Prediction with Bayesian Optimization
projects.award: Published in Springer Algorithms for Intelligent Systems
projects.description: Research project exploring how external factors like weather, peak hours, and seasonal holidays affect urban traffic flow prediction. Implemented a Prophet additive regression model with Bayesian hyperparameter optimization to capture stochastic patterns traditional models miss. Showed that incorporating exogenous factors meaningfully improved forecast accuracy across weekends and holidays. Co-authored and published in Springer Algorithms for Intelligent Systems.
